title: Gangamma Temple, Dhenuvakonda
tagline: Gangamma Temple, Dhenuvakonda — spiritual town of Andhra Pradesh

overview.short: Goddess Gangamma Temple is in the outskirts of Dhenuva Konda, Garlapadu village in Prakasam District of Andhra Pradesh, India.
overview.description: Goddess Gangamma Temple is in the outskirts of Dhenuva Konda, Garlapadu village in Prakasam District of Andhra Pradesh, India. Sunday is preferable day to offer pooja in the temple. Goddess Gangamma is a powerful figure to the Yadava community. They celebrate Jatara for her on the full moon in April.

topPlaces.name: Dhenuva Konda
topPlaces.description: Dhenuva Konda is a village in Addanki mandal of Prakasam district in the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh.

topPlaces.name: Doddavaram
topPlaces.description: Doddavaram is located in the Maddipadu Mandal of Prakasam District in Andhra Pradesh, India. It is said that this village was known as Veera Narasimha Puri Agraharam during the reign of Addanki rulers.

topPlaces.name: Tellapadu
topPlaces.description: Tellapadu (pronounced "Tellabadu") is a village of Doddavaram Panchayathi in Maddipadu Mandal of Prakasam District in the southern Indian state of Andhra Pradesh. It is a part of Bapatla (SC) Parliamentary constituency and Santhanuthalapadu (SC) assembly constituency.

topPlaces.name: Annangi
topPlaces.description: Annangi is a village located in Maddipadu Mandal of Prakasam district, Andhra Pradesh, India This village in located on the banks of the Gundlakamma river.

topPlaces.name: Manikeswaram
topPlaces.description: Manikeswaram is a village on the banks of the Gundlakamma river in the Prakasam district of the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh.

topPlaces.name: Gundlakamma Reservoir Project
topPlaces.description: Gundlakamma Reservoir Project (officially Kandula Obula Reddy Gundlakamma Reservoir Project) is an irrigation project across the Gundlakamma river located in Prakasam district in Andhra Pradesh, India. The dam, with 3.859 billion cubic feet of live storage capacity.

topPlaces.name: Medarametla
topPlaces.description: Medarametla is a village situated in Bapatla district.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Gangamma Temple, Dhenuvakonda?
faq.a: The best time to visit Gangamma Temple, Dhenuvakonda is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Gangamma Temple, Dhenuvakonda?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Vijayawada Airport (129 km) and the nearest railway station is Guntur Junction (85 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Gangamma Temple, Dhenuvakonda?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Gangamma Temple, Dhenuvakonda at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Gangamma Temple, Dhenuvakonda?
faq.a: Stays in Gangamma Temple, Dhenuvakonda start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Gangamma Temple, Dhenuvakonda?
faq.a: The top attractions are Dhenuva Konda, Doddavaram, Tellapadu, Annangi, Manikeswaram.
